methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $231M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $2.7B.

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Pragmatic Semiconductor is a Series D-stage chip design company that has raised $231M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 3 private companies at Series C/D stage in the semiconductor sector, with reference to 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 additional private frontier-tech firms in adjacent markets including Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, and Etched.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $231M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement, yields $346M. Peer-median analysis across the 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Tenstorrent ($2.7B at Series D), d-Matrix ($2.0B at Series C), and DEEPX ($529M at Series C)—produces a median valuation of $2.0B, with a range spanning $529M to $2.7B. The two methods reflect materially different market signals, with stage-progression rooted in capital consumed and peer-median capturing recent private market pricing for comparable-stage semiconductor investments.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $295M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.2B. This wide band reflects genuine uncertainty: stage-progression and peer-median diverge substantially, signaling either that Pragmatic commands a premium to capital-deployed metrics or that peer valuations embed high-risk assumptions. Resolution hinges on technology differentiation, path to revenue, and underlying unit economics relative to the peer cohort.
